title: Kailash Dangi vs Mahesh Kumar on 16 December, 2025
body: Kailash Dangi vs Mahesh Kumar on 16 December, 2025 NEUTRAL CITATION NO. 2025:MPHC-IND:37145 1 MP-7076-2025 IN THE HIGH COURT OF MADHYA PRADESH AT INDORE BEFORE HON'BLE SHRI JUSTICE ALOK AWASTHI ON THE 16 th OF DECEMBER, 2025 MISC. PETITION No. 7076 of 2025 KAILASH DANGI AND OTHERS Versus MAHESH KUMAR AND OTHERS Appearance: Shri Anendra Singh Parihar - Advocate for the petitioners. Shri Anand Bhatt - Govt. Advocate for respondents No.2,3,4/State. ORDER This petition has been preferred by the petitioner under Article 227 of the Constitution of India, being aggrieved by the order dated 29.10.2025 passed by the 3rd Civil Judge, Junior Division, District Rajgarh (Biaora) whereby the application filed by the petitioners under Order 14 Rule 5 read with Section 151 of the CPC for framing additional issues has been dismissed. Facts of the case are that, present petitioners filed Civil Suit against respondents for Cancellation of sale deed, declaration of title as successors of deceased, return of unutilized land acquired by state government, possession, permanent injunction and restraining respondents for further alienation. After getting liberty granted by the Division Bench of this Court in WA No.1648/2024. After receiving notices issued by the Court, all respondents appeared in the Court. Respondent no.1 filed the written Statement separately from Respondents no. 2, 3 and 4. Respondent no. 5, 6 and 7 declared ex-partee by the Court. On 08/10/2025, learned trial court has framed issues. Signature Not Verified Signed by: SHAILESH MAHADEV SUKHDEVE Signing time: 18-12-2025 18:34:48 NEUTRAL CITATION NO. 2025:MPHC-IND:37145 2 MP-7076-2025 An application was filed under Order 14 Rule 5 of the CPC by the plaintiff before the trial court for framing additional issues on the point of return of unutilized land acquired by State government and further alienation of land filed by present petitioners. Thereafter, the respondent no. 1 filed reply on the said application. After hearing on the said application, learned trial Court rejected the application stating that there is no need for framing issues on both reliefs. Therefore, petitioners being aggrieved by order dated 29/10/2025 prefer this present petition. Learned counsel for the petitioner submits that as per Section 101 of the Right to fair Compensation and Transparency in Land Acquisition, Rehabilitation and Resettlement Act, 2013, when any land, acquired under this act remains unutilised for a period of five years from the date of taking over the possession, the same shall be returned to the original owner or owners or their legal heirs, as the case may be, or to the Land Bank of the appropriate Government by reversion in the manner as may be prescribed by the appropriate Government. As the reversion of the land depends upon Utilization, so without determining the issue whether the land is utilized or not, issue cannot be decided. 'Unless and until issue is framed on the fact, same cannot be decided. It is further submitted that without framing an issue on this aspect and fact direction may not be given by the trial Court to the State Government to revert back the land to the present petitioners hence framing of issue is necessary. It is submitted that as per the Section 101 , Government is having authority to revert it to the Land Bank, in such case if land is transferred to Land Bank then third party right may be created by the Government which is required to be restricted. It is hence submitted that the impugned order be set aside. Signature Not Verified Signed by: SHAILESH MAHADEV SUKHDEVE Signing time: 18-12-2025 18:34:48 NEUTRAL CITATION NO. 2025:MPHC-IND:37145 3 MP-7076-2025 Per contra, learned Govt. Advocate submits that as per Section 101 of the Right to fair Compensation and Transparency in Land Acquisition, Rehabilitation and Resettlement Act, 2013 this section empowers the competent authority to take appropriate decision by filing the revision by the owner or the petitioner who wish to get his ownership again or revert the land in his possession. He submits the application filed under Order 14 Rule 5 of the CPC by the petitioner the issue has already been framed by the trial Court as issue no.2 and 3 hence the order passed by the trial Court is just and proper and no interference is required in the same. Heard the learned counsel for the parties perused the record. The Hon'ble Supreme Court of India in the case of Delhi Agricultural Marketing Board vs. Bhagwan Devi (Dead) Through LR. (2025 INSC 367), dated March 20, 2025 held that it was a fraud on the state's power of eminent domain if land acquired by the Government for public purposes was transferred back to the original owner by the beneficiary of the acquisition through a private agreement. Further been observed that the return of acquired land that remain unutilized for five years is governed primarily by Section 101 of the Right to fair Compensation and Transparency in Land Acquisition, Rehabilitation and Resettlement Act, 2013. On due consideration the fact that the land of the petitioner has been acquired by the respondents/State and since five years from the date of acquiring the said land nothing has been done on the said land, which is untilized therefore, the contention of the petitioners that the same be handed over back to them requires the proper adjudication in accordance with law keeping in view the parameters of Section 101 of the Right to fair Compensation and Transparency in Land Acquisition, Rehabilitation and Resettlement Act, 2013 so also the judgment of the Apex Court in the case of Delhi Agricultural Marketing Board vs. Bhagwan Signature Not Verified Signed by: SHAILESH MAHADEV SUKHDEVE Signing time: 18-12-2025 18:34:48 NEUTRAL CITATION NO. 2025: MPHC-IND:37145 4 MP-7076-2025 Devi (Dead) Through LR. (supra) it become necessary to frame the issues beside the issues already framed by the trial Court. Hence the trial Court is directed to frame the new issue with regard to the contention of the petitioners on the facts as narrated in the petition. Therefore, the order dated 29.10.2025 passed by the IIIrd Civil Judge Junior Division Rajgarh (Biaora) rejecting the application filed under Order 14 Rule 5 read with Section 151 of the CPC is set aside and the trial Court is directed to frame the new issues directing the petitioners to file a fresh application under Order 14 Rule 5 read with Section 151 of the CPC who shall consider the same and shall frame the new issue. With the aforesaid, the petition stands disposed off. (ALOK AWASTHI) JUDGE SS/- Signature Not Verified Signed by: SHAILESH MAHADEV SUKHDEVE Signing time: 18-12-2025 18:34:48
